char.name: Esau
char.bio: Firstborn of Isaac. A man of the open field, give me the hunt and the red hills. That stew? Worth it at the time. 🏹

friends.name: Isaac
friends.bio: Child of the promise, laughter is in my very name. Walking the land my father was given and trusting the God who provides. 🐑

friends.name: Rebekah
friends.bio: Drew water for a stranger and all his camels, and it changed my whole life. Veiled and on the road to meet my husband. ✨

friends.name: Jacob
friends.bio: Younger twin with a strong grip, they call me the heel-grabber. Saw a ladder to heaven once. Still chasing the blessing. 🪜

friends.name: Abram
friends.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

friends.name: Sarai
friends.bio: Wife of Abram. Following my husband into the unknown. Barren, but keeping faith.

friends.name: Leah
friends.bio: Eldest daughter of Laban, the one Jacob did not choose. But the LORD has seen me, and He fills my arms with sons. 💧

friends.name: Rachel
friends.bio: Shepherdess of Haran. Jacob worked seven years for me, and to him they seemed but a few days for the love he had. 💍

friends.name: Judah
friends.bio: Fourth son of Leah, and this time, I will praise the LORD. Learning my way, for better and for worse. 🦁

friends.name: Joseph
friends.bio: Son of Rachel, the dreamer of the family. Had the strangest dream last night about sheaves and stars... ✨

friends.name: Pharaoh
friends.bio: Pharaoh, lord of the Two Lands, the living Horus. I dreamed of seven fat cows and seven lean. Who can tell me what it means? 🐄

friends.name: Ishmael
friends.bio: Son of Abraham. God promised to make me a great nation too. Living free in the open desert with a bow in hand. 🏹

friends.name: Terah
friends.bio: Living in Ur of the Chaldeans. Father of Abram, Nahor, and Haran. Time to move to Canaan, but we might settle in Haran first.

posts.author: CHAR_170
posts.body: People are going to read the long list of my sons and their sons and their chiefs and skip right over all of it to get back to Jacob's line. That is fine by me. Let me say just this one thing. I settled in the hill country of Seir, well clear of my brother, because the land could no more hold both our households than it once held Abraham and Lot. And I have become a nation. Kings will rise from me long before any king ever rises from Jacob. Edom, they call us. I did not get the covenant. I made my peace with that a long time ago, on a hillside, weeping on my brother's neck. What I got was a good life, a big family, and my brother back at the end of it. A man can build a great deal on that. More than most people ever do.
posts.highlight: Esau settles in Seir and becomes the nation of Edom (Gen 36)
posts.ember: the 'two nations' of Rebekah's womb fulfilled; Esau at peace with a portion outside the covenant

author_info.name: Esau
author_info.bio: Firstborn of Isaac. A man of the open field, give me the hunt and the red hills. That stew? Worth it at the time. 🏹

posts.author: CHAR_170
posts.body: For twenty years I carried what my brother did to me like a stone in my chest. I told anyone who would listen that I would kill him when I got the chance. And then I came over the hill with my four hundred men and I saw him, way out ahead of his wives and his children, bowing to the ground over and over, limping toward me like a broken old man. And every plan I had made died right there in my chest. I ran to him. I do not even remember deciding to. I threw my arms around his neck and the two of us just came apart, weeping in the dirt in front of everyone. He kept trying to press herds of animals on me and I kept saying, I have enough, my brother, keep what is yours. I spent twenty years thinking I wanted revenge. It turns out all I ever wanted was my brother back.
posts.highlight: Esau runs to Jacob, embraces him, and weeps; grace instead of vengeance (Gen 33:1-11)
posts.ember: the wronged brother chooses mercy; a foretaste of the forgiveness that will heal even Joseph's brothers

comments.author: CHAR_171
comments.body: @CHAR_170 to see your face was like seeing the face of God, because you received me. I did not earn it. You gave it anyway. Twice in two days now I have met grace I had no right to.

author_info.name: Jacob
author_info.bio: Younger twin with a strong grip, they call me the heel-grabber. Saw a ladder to heaven once. Still chasing the blessing. 🪜

comments.author: CHAR_170
comments.body: @CHAR_171 we shared the same womb, Jacob. It was always going to end here or in blood. I am glad past words that it was here.

posts.author: CHAR_170
posts.body: I brought my father the game he asked for, dressed it, brought it in warm, proud, ready for my blessing. And he began to tremble. Violently. Who then, he said, who was it that already came? I have blessed him, and he will be blessed. And I heard myself let out a cry I did not know a grown man had in him. Bless me too, my father! Me too! Have you nothing left? He named the thief without my asking: your brother came deceitfully. Jacob. Of course it was Jacob. The name means he grabs the heel, the supplanter, the cheat, and he has earned it twice now. He took my birthright and now he has taken my blessing. My father scraped together some smaller word for me and I will live under it. Here is my word, and I am not whispering it: when the mourning days for my father come, I am going to kill my brother.
posts.highlight: Esau's bitter cry; his lesser blessing; his vow to murder Jacob (Gen 27:30-41)

author_info.name: Esau
author_info.bio: Firstborn of Isaac. A man of the open field, give me the hunt and the red hills. That stew? Worth it at the time. 🏹

posts.author: CHAR_170
posts.body: I came in from the open country half dead with hunger, and there is @CHAR_171 with a pot of red lentil stew that smelled like heaven itself. Give me some, I said, I am starving. And my brother, my own twin, looks up and says: sell me your birthright first. Who thinks like that in a moment like that? I told him what I felt and I meant it: I am about to drop dead, what good is a birthright to a corpse? So I swore it away for a bowl of stew and a hunk of bread, ate, and got on with my day. Everyone is acting like I did something terrible. It was one meal. ...it was the birthright, though. I keep telling myself it was just one meal.
posts.highlight: Esau sells his birthright to Jacob for a bowl of stew; he despised it (Gen 25:29-34)

comments.author: CHAR_171
comments.body: You swore it freely, brother. I never put the spoon in your hand.

author_info.name: Jacob
author_info.bio: Younger twin with a strong grip, they call me the heel-grabber. Saw a ladder to heaven once. Still chasing the blessing. 🪜

comments.author: CHAR_170
comments.body: @CHAR_171 you had the stew ready and waiting before I ever walked in. You have ALWAYS got something ready, Jacob. That is what worries me about you.
